diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse abdominal pain and vomiting are classic features of adrenal crisis and mimic an acute abdomen.
Non-focal, diffuse pain is atypical for appendicitis, which is usually localising.
Nausea/vomiting and anorexia are cardinal in crisis and drive further volume depletion.
The commonest benign explanation — a red herring that must not close off the shock assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound generalised weakness, bedbound, feeling of fading away

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Overwhelming asthenia and near-collapse reflect cortisol deficiency and shock physiology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive altered mental status (drowsy, only briefly rousable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced conscious level marks critical hypoperfusion and/or hyponatraemia — a decompensation red flag mandating emergency care.

ACTH-driven hyperpigmentation is characteristic of PRIMARY adrenal insufficiency and reframes undifferentiated shock as adrenal crisis. Patient/carer-observable, so admissible via telehealth (unverified).

symptoms.symptom_name: Months of extreme fatigue and feeling run down

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic fatigue is a common prodrome of insidious adrenal insufficiency.
A febrile illness is a classic precipitant of crisis in someone with failing adrenal reserve.
Fever with shock raises sepsis, which may be the precipitant and coexist.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pallor, cold clammy peripheries and tachypnoea (carer-observed poor perfusion)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral shutdown and compensatory tachypnoea are carer-observable proxies for shock in the absence of measured vitals.
Objective hypotension refractory to fluids alone — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers shock from carer-observable signs, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia with weak peripheral pulses and capillary refill > 4 seconds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective markers of hypoperfusion — clinician-obtained, sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse abdominal tenderness without focal peritonism or guarding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of focal peritonism argues against a primary surgical abdomen.
Diffuse tenderness is consistent with the crisis abdominal presentation but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal bedside capillary blood glucose (5.2 mmol/L)

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: DKA not excluded as a cause of vomiting/abdominal pain/altered consciousness.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal glucose makes DKA very unlikely and removes it from the leading differentials.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The characteristic biochemical signature of primary adrenal insufficiency is missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyponatraemia with hyperkalaemia is the classic electrolyte pattern of aldosterone/cortisol deficiency — hospital investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Adrenal crisis on undiagnosed primary adrenal insufficiency
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Refractory hypotension + profound weakness + altered consciousness + hyperpigmentation + anticipated hyponatraemia/hyperkalaemia together are strongly specific for adrenal crisis and constitute the sealed answer-key tetrad.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer (000) — do not delay for investigations
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Decompensated shock with altered consciousness needs emergency resuscitation; no test should delay escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum electrolytes (sodium, potassium), urea and creatinine
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reveals the hyponatraemia/hyperkalaemia of adrenal insufficiency and guides resuscitation — obtained in hospital, not via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Paired serum cortisol and ACTH (before or with first hydrocortisone dose)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms primary adrenal insufficiency; must not delay treatment.
Rapidly excludes DKA/hypoglycaemia as a cause of vomiting and altered consciousness.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Septic screen (cultures, lactate) and treat precipitant
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The febrile illness that triggered the crisis is sought and treated in parallel.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Oral rehydration trial at home before escalation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Contraindicated — ongoing vomiting with drowsiness makes oral intake an aspiration risk and cannot correct the deficit; escalate instead.
